methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Mujin is a Series C robotics and manufacturing automation company that has raised $85M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, comprising 8 private firms (including Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, and Dexterity) and 1 public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology, using total raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $144M. Peer-median analysis across the 9-company cohort yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in robotics subsectors and capital efficiency; notably, Hadrian trades at the median ($1.6B at Series C), while earlier-stage 1X Technologies commands $10.0B.

The triangulated analysis produces a range of $123M (low), $872M (median), and $1.8B (high). The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound conservatively; peer comparables drive the upper bound. Key uncertainties include: capital efficiency and burn rate relative to peers; product-market traction and commercialization timeline; and whether Mujin's specific robotics subsector commands premium or discount valuations versus the broader cohort. The $872M median represents a reasonable central estimate, though wide dispersion ($1.6B peer median vs. $144M stage-based) indicates material estimation risk pending deeper operational diligence.
